That’s a Tough Question

Created by Viktoriia Pyniv

AI-assisted educational resource developed with ChatGPT (OpenAI).

Resource: ESL Brains — That’s a tough question

Section 1

Difficult questions

Recognise what makes a question uncomfortable, complex or difficult to answer.

1What makes a question tough?

Decide which questions might be difficult to answer and explain why.

  • How much do you earn?
  • Is patriotism always a good thing?
  • Are you pregnant?
  • Why are you still single?
  • How does quantum computing actually work?
  • Don’t you think your generation is too dependent on technology?

2Create tough questions

Choose several situations and create two difficult questions for each.

job interviewfamily dinnerdatebusiness meetingcelebrity interviewsports press conference
A. Have you ever been asked one?
B. Could it sound acceptable in a different situation?
C. How would you react: laughter, humour, silence, honesty, annoyance or avoidance?
Section 2

Watch and respond

Predict the athlete’s reaction, notice the language she uses and discuss how she handles the question.

3Predict the reaction

Watch the beginning and discuss why the question makes Eileen Gu laugh.

4Complete the phrases

  1. I think that’s an answer itself.
  2. So, the “two medals lost” situation, to be with you, I think is kind of a ridiculous perspective to take.
  3. I think that is more than , but thank you.

5Discuss the response

  • What did you think of the interviewer’s question?
  • What did you think of the way the athlete handled it?
  • Should reporters follow any rules when asking difficult questions?
  • How can people deal with questions they do not feel comfortable answering?
Section 3

Understand and practise

Work out what the expressions do, organise them by function and use them in short spoken responses.

6Meaning

Imagine you hear these expressions for the first time. What do you think each speaker is trying to do?

Click a phrase on the left, then click its matching meaning on the right.

7Mini check

Read one situation at a time, choose the most natural opening and check your answer.

1 / 8

8Function

Drag each phrase into the correct communicative function.

9Situation

Choose a function. You have five seconds to begin your response with a suitable phrase.

5seconds
Choose a function to start.
Section 4

Opinion Battle

Use the expressions in a sustained conversation and respond directly to your partner’s ideas.

10Keep the debate going

Work in pairs. Choose a statement and discuss it for two minutes. Every response must begin with one of today’s expressions. Try to use at least four different expressions.

Topic 1 / 10
